required_complexity_patterns.notes: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Reference DOM (Custom div dropdown): Location picked from custom div dropdown, not native select Reference DOM (Triple nested fieldsets): Fieldset inside fieldset inside fieldset with legends at each level Reference DOM (Concatenated label words): Teamtailor-style bug: "first namerequired" with no space before required Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: custom-combobox, pill-yes-no, aria-label-only, decoy-form, label-wraps-deep. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Custom div dropdown; Triple nested fieldsets; Concatenated label words.
